purpose: Public evidence for reconstructing a detailed personalized daily productivity/activity breakdown.

notes: Do not collapse activities into broad categories unless strongly supported by evidence.
Use cross-signal evidence across browsing, location, calls, todos, and user heuristics.
Chrome history may contain duplicates, redirects, or shallow navigational noise; merged sessions are provided as an additional hint rather than final truth.

todo_items.text: review a research paper and prepare for a project build
visit a startup contact in another city

heuristics: google maps are used to plan future trips
intent of users action often can be inferred by name of chat with llms used during activity

events_original.description: traveled to meet a startup contact in another city to discuss their company
visited a startup office in another city
traveled from another city to a grocery store
traveled from the grocery store to a coffee shop
traveled from the coffee shop to a coworking space
traveled from the coworking space to home

events_rounded_15m.description: traveled to meet a startup contact in another city to discuss their company
visited a startup office in another city
traveled from another city to a grocery store
traveled from the grocery store to a coffee shop
traveled from the coffee shop to a coworking space
traveled from the coworking space to home

cleanup_notes: Source CSV was kept unchanged; this file is a derived normalized artifact.
Redirect-like domains such as t.co are retained in raw events but domain-normalized when they clearly point to YouTube content.
Repeated identical entries are removed only when time, title, and normalized domain all match exactly.
Merged sessions are intended as higher-level evidence, not final labels.
